subject: Chemistry
title: Fraud and Fake Goldmakers
description: Learn Fraud and Fake Goldmakers in one minute.

hook.text: Many alchemists were con artists. They rigged demonstrations with hidden gold to fool wealthy patrons.

buildup.text: Common tricks included hollow stirring rods filled with gold dust and crucibles with gold hidden under false bottoms.

discovery.text: Edward Kelley, assistant to John Dee, convinced the Holy Roman Emperor he could make gold. He was eventually imprisoned.

twist.text: In 1404, England passed a law making it a felony to 'multiply' gold or silver. Fraudulent alchemists faced execution.

climax.text: German alchemist Johann Böttger was imprisoned by Augustus the Strong to make gold. Instead, he invented European porcelain.

punchline.text: Fake gold, real prison. One imprisoned alchemist made porcelain instead.

quiz.question: What did imprisoned alchemist Johann Böttger accidentally invent?
